But if you do want to look at the benchmarks here, you can see that M2.7 is here versus GP5.4 and Opus, right? So for example, if you look at SWE bench pro, which is software engineering, you can see M2.7 scores 56.2 versus Opus 57.3 and GPT 5.4, which is 57.7. What this basically means is like the difference between some of the best models in the world, an M2.7 is is tiny and marginal, right? The same with, for example, like if you look at 5 Pro, they're scoring equally on the benchmarks versus Opus 4.6. OPUS 4.6 scored 55.6 on Vip Pro. M2.7 scored 55.6 on by Pro, right? So, they're basically even. Now, this is a self-improvening system. What that means is it iterates on itself, right? So, it can build on itself. It can analyze what it's doing. It uses an iteration process as well to improve and evolve its own potential. So it should get better and better over time which is pretty cool as well. So amazing stuff. You can use it for free. It's an AI super agent. And just to recap here, what is difference between these? Right. Well, basically with this whole process with Zo computer plus Miniax M2.7, you basically got a super smart AI assistant on your computer, right? So it's an AI agent that can improve itself while sleep. And when the two tools come together, aka Zo computer and M2.7, you get something that's free and incredibly powerful, right? So Miniax M2.7 Miniax is basically a big AI company based in Shanghai, China, right? They make AI models kind of like brains behind chatbots and AI tools. And their newest model is called M2.7. So it's not just a smart AI. It's an AI that helps build the next smarter version of itself. It's self iterating. What does self-improving AI actually mean? Well, think of it like this, right? A normal AI answers your questions. A self-improving AI answers your questions and improves itself at the same time, right? So Miniax M2.7 is the first model deeply participating in its own evolution. So for example, when they were developing M2.7, they let the model update its own memory and build dozens of complex skills to help with reinforcement learning experiments and then improve its own learning process based on those results. In plain English, that means the AI helped write the rules that made it smarter. That's never really been done before at this scale. Now, if you're wondering like, how does it actually improve itself? That sounds crazy. Well, here's a cycle that it runs through. A researcher starts by discussing an experimental idea with the agent who helps with literature review, tracks a preset experiment specification, pipelines, data, and other artifacts and launches experiments. During the experiments, the agent monitors its own progress and automatically triggers log reading, debugging, metric analysis, code fixes, merge requests, and smoke tests, identifying and configurating like subtle key differences. Now these tasks could have required the collaboration of multiple human researchers from different teams before but now human resource researchers only interact for critical decisions and discussions. To sum that up in plain English, a job that used to need a whole team of humans now runs mostly on itself. Now what is Zo computer? So Zo computer is your own personal cloud computer powered by AI. So it's like having a tiny powerful computer that floats in the sky. Zo is an intelligent cloud computer that lets you turn your ideas into reality. So, it can store files, connect tools, and then research and build practically anything like you've seen, like apps, websites, you can schedule tasks, whatever. And the cool thing about this is it runs 24/7, even whilst you sleep. You can schedule meetings, you can manage your inbox, and it builds apps and websites in minutes. You can even email and text it from your phone. Now, you might be wondering, why is this free? Well, they're just free until the end of March, right? So, Miniax M2.7 and Kim K2.5 are free on Zo until the end of March. There's a limited window, but that is a big deal, right? and so also increase the AI usage plans on their free plan significantly. What do you get on the free plan? You get about 100 GB of free storage, access to Frontier models um and that sort of thing. You can also connect all your best integrations into there and you can bring your own API key if you want to as well. The other cool thing about this is like some people worry about security with openclaw. So with Zo, it performs regular snapshots to your computer storing the entire state at a point in time on the Zo system storage. So you can restore your computer back to any point as well.
